inlineContent.text: The KeyboardKit
engine makes it easy to style an entire keyboard with a single view modifier. KeyboardKit Pro has many predefined themes, and lets you create your own custom themes as well.
👑 KeyboardKit Pro unlocks a theme engine for all licenses that include the
feature. You can sign up for KeyboardKit Pro from the
The
developer guide has step-by-step instructions on how to create custom
values for the theme engine.
KeyboardKit has a
type that is also a namespace for theme-related types and views, like
and

class with observable state and auto-persisted theme
, that can be used to get and set the current theme.

You can get all predefined themes and style variations with the
propery. You can also create completely custom themes.
You can apply any pre-defined or custom theme with the
modifier. Use try? to fallback to the standard theme:
Although you can use the
and
to set a theme, you must still apply it manually.
KeyboardKit has a

The context
is used by default. KeyboardKit Pro unlocks a feature-specific
that can be used to handle these settings.
KeyboardKit Pro unlocks
-related settings views that make it easier to manage theme settings in an app or keyboard:
The
template will automatically link to this screen if the current license unlocks the
feature.
The screen list available themes in shelves, and will by default set the main, persisted
when a theme is tapped.
